Scene mapping confirmation and auto-ignite wait: This GameObject has a BoxCollider (enabled, non-trigger) and no Rigidbody. The attached MonoBehaviour component (m_Script guid: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d; fields match: timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, totalHitTime=5, mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095, onNeighborsThreshold=2) is the Fire script provided. No m_Calls (UnityEvent) are configured on this object. During this wait, Update() can auto-run and, if onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold is satisfied by scene neighbors, CheckNeighborState() will set shouldTurnOn and the timer will elapse to isOn=true, calling UpdateFireState(true) which plays the particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.

Extinguish sequence when isOn is true: This step assumes an external water projectile/system will contact this object's BoxCollider and invoke IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) repeatedly within maxHitInterval=2s to accumulate currentHitTime >= totalHitTime=5s. That path exercises isTurningOff logic in Update(), transitions fire off, and calls UpdateFireState(true). No m_Calls are defined, and Fire.WaterHit takes a parameter, so we cannot directly invoke it via a no-arg Trigger; collisions must occur via gameplay.

With the fire currently ON from the previous step, simulate a single water hit via the IWaterInteractable by invoking WaterHit(Vector3) once at t=0, then no further hits for > maxHitInterval (2s). Expected: isTurningOff is set true at hit, currentHitInterval accumulates and exceeds 2s, which resets isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime=0 without turning the fire off.

From ON state, apply repeated water hits so that WaterHit(Vector3) is called at least every 1.0s (< maxHitInterval=2s) for a cumulative duration >= totalHitTime (5s). Expected: currentHitTime reaches 5s while currentHitInterval stays <2s, causing fire to turn OFF in Update: isOn=false, shouldTurnOn=false, timeToOnDelta=0, isTurningOff=false, currentHitTime=0, UpdateFireState(true) which stops all ParticleSystems and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1).

Extinguishing via water hits. This object implements IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) (called externally on water collision). During this trigger window, repeatedly hit the collider with the water stream so that each hit occurs within maxHitInterval (2s) to keep isTurningOff true; when cumulative currentHitTime reaches totalHitTime (5s), Fire turns off and UpdateFireState(true) stops the particle system and notifies neighbors and FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: There are no no-arg public methods to call directly; interaction must be via physics/water system invoking WaterHit.

Cancel-turning-off branch. After calling WaterHit once (sets isTurningOff=true and resets currentHitInterval to 0), allow a gap >= maxHitInterval (2s) without additional WaterHit calls. Expect Update() to set isTurningOff=false and currentHitTime reset to 0 (extinguish attempt canceled).

Neighbor threshold drop branch. Ensure onNeighbors < onNeighborsThreshold (2) (e.g., neighbors turn off or move out of maxNeighborDistance=3.5). Expect CheckNeighborState() to set shouldTurnOn=false and timeToOnDelta=0. This covers the else-branch preventing ignition when insufficient neighbors are on.

The Fire script exposes no public parameterless methods; its interactions require: neighbor notifications (NotifyNeighborState(bool)), water hits (WaterHit(Vector3)), manual state toggles (SetIsOn(bool)), or neighbor discovery (FindNeighbors(Fire[],int)). To achieve code coverage (turn on via neighbors and turn off via repeated WaterHit within maxHitInterval=2 until totalHitTime=5), external systems or additional scene info are required to invoke these methods or to provide neighboring Fire instances and a water system.

GameObject has BoxCollider (no Rigidbody), so Trigger is allowed. Attached MonoBehaviour (GUID: 1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) matches the provided Fire script and uses exact serialized settings from scene (debug=0, isOn=0, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, mainEffectGo=1849464941111748095, neighbors=[]). However, this Fire script exposes no public parameterless methods (NotifyNeighborState(bool), UpdateFireState(bool), WaterHit(Vector3), SetIsOn(bool), FindNeighbors(Fire[],int) all require parameters), and there are no UnityEvents (m_Calls) listed for this object. This Trigger step interacts with the collider to exercise Awake/Update lifecycles but cannot directly invoke script methods. To achieve full coverage we will need either: (1) a parameterless wrapper method or UnityEvent to call WaterHit/SetIsOn/UpdateFireState, (2) populated neighbors or a controller that calls FindNeighbors/NotifyNeighborState, or (3) details of the water system that invokes IWaterInteractable.WaterHit.

MonoBehaviour_0 maps to Fire (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) on GameObject 'Fire-Wrapper - R - 2 - 2'. BoxCollider enabled, no Rigidbody. No UnityEvent m_Calls exposed. Serialized: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, onNeighborsThreshold=2, maxNeighborDistance=4, downMultiplier=2, totalHitTime=5. This trigger is a timed wait to allow automatic Update() behavior when the neighbor condition is satisfied (onNeighbors >= 2) so shouldTurnOn becomes true and the fire turns on after timeToOn elapses. Expected behavior upon auto ignition: UpdateFireState(true) => ParticleSystem.Play; NotifyAllNeighbors(); FireController.Instance.FireChanged(+1). Requires: at least two neighbor Fire instances within 4 units and already on; or that FindNeighbors/neighbor wiring be completed externally.

Turn-off path: while isOn==true, repeated WaterHit(Vector3) calls must occur so currentHitInterval < maxHitInterval (2s) until currentHitTime >= totalHitTime (5s), then Update() turns fire off and calls UpdateFireState(true) => ParticleSystem.Stop; NotifyAllNeighbors(); FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: Fire has no public parameterless methods and no UnityEvent m_Calls; WaterHit(Vector3) requires a parameter and cannot be invoked via a generic Trigger per constraints. A water system or test hook must invoke Fire.WaterHit(Vector3) repeatedly during this 5.5s window.

Validate Fire MonoBehaviour settings and exercise the sustained-water-hit turn-off path. This GameObject has a BoxCollider (m_IsTrigger=0) and no Rigidbody. The attached MonoBehaviour (m_Script guid=1729fd01c90a64d5b8037e1eef0d000d) matches Fire.cs with serialized values: isOn=false, timeToOnMin=40, timeToOnMax=60, downMultiplier=2, maxHitInterval=2, totalHitTime=5, onNeighborsThreshold=2, minNeighborDownDistance=3, mainEffectGo fileID=1849464941111748095. Action simulates continuous water impacts that should cause IWaterInteractable.WaterHit(Vector3) to be invoked repeatedly within intervals < maxHitInterval until totalHitTime is reached, at which point Update() turns the fire off (isOn=false), resets turning-off timers, stops particles via UpdateFireState(true), notifies neighbors, and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(-1). Note: Fire exposes no public no-parameter methods; thus no direct method calls are specified here.

Exercise the cancellation path of turning off. After at least one WaterHit has set isTurningOff=true, cease hits for >= maxHitInterval (2s). Expected: in Update(), currentHitInterval exceeds maxHitInterval; isTurningOff becomes false and currentHitTime resets to 0, leaving the fire state unchanged (remains whatever it was prior to the pause). No public no-parameter methods exist to call directly.

Exercise neighbor-driven ignition. Preconditions needed: neighbors list populated (via Fire.FindNeighbors or manual serialization) with >=2 neighbors within maxNeighborDistance=4, where at least one neighbor is sufficiently below (transform.y difference > minNeighborDownDistance=3) to allow downMultiplier acceleration. When onNeighbors >= onNeighborsThreshold and this fire is off and not already turning on, CheckNeighborState() should set shouldTurnOn=true and accumulate timeToOnDelta until reaching timeToOn (Random[40,60] scaled by downMultiplier if hasDownNeighbor). On reaching timeToOn, isOn becomes true and UpdateFireState(true) plays particle systems and calls FireController.Instance.FireChanged(1). This step represents the time passage and neighbor state; no direct public no-parameter methods are available to invoke.
